title: Archidendron gogolense
taxonomy: Archidendron gogolense is classified as a species of legume in the family Fabaceae. Specifically, it belongs to the genus Archidendron and is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Island. The species is further classified into the tribe Ingeae, which includes several other species of trees and shrubs. Archidendron gogolense is often found growing in coastal and island regions, where it can thrive in a variety of conditions. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and adaptations to its native environment.
uses: Archidendron gogolense, a plant native to the tropical regions of Australia, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Its extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. The plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties make it a potential remedy for wound healing and skin infections. Additionally, its leaves and bark have been used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues and sore throats. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Archidendron gogolense for medicinal use.

parts: Archidendron gogolense, also known as the Gogol's tree or the tropical copal tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. intro: Archidendron gogolense, also known as Gogole's tree or Gogole's bean tree, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Archidendron gogolense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Archidendron gogolense is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the preparation of topical creams and ointments.
